subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B8.1.1.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of puberty rites in their ethnic community and compare with other Ghanaian cultures
indicator_description: Explain what puberty rites are and how they are performed in their community.
examplars:
1. Explain what puberty rites are. [cite: 52]
2. Discuss the importance of puberty rites in their community. [cite: 52]
3. Describe the processes involved in puberty rites in their community. [cite: 52]
4. Discuss the items used during the performance of puberty rites in their community. [cite: 52]

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B8.1.1.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of puberty rites in their ethnic community and compare with other Ghanaian cultures
indicator_description: Discuss the significance of puberty rites and their relevance to the individual and the society.
examplars:
1. Identify and discuss the significance of puberty rites for the individual and the community. [cite: 52]
2. Discuss the educational value of puberty rites. [cite: 52]
3. Discuss the relevance of puberty rites in modern society. [cite: 52]

subject: Ghanaian Language
sub_strand: NAMING SYSTEMS — FAMILY NAMES, KINSHIP TERMS, PROVERBIAL AND INSINUATION NAMES
content_standard: B8.1.2.1 Exhibit understanding of family names and kinship terms and their relationship in the naming system
indicator_description: Identify and explain the origins of family names in their community.
examplars:
1. Identify some family names in your community and explain their origins. [cite: 52]
2. Discuss the importance of family names as a source of identity. [cite: 52]
3. Discuss how family names reflect the history of the community. [cite: 52]

subject: Ghanaian Language
sub_strand: NAMING SYSTEMS — FAMILY NAMES, KINSHIP TERMS, PROVERBIAL AND INSINUATION NAMES
content_standard: B8.1.2.1 Exhibit understanding of family names and kinship terms and their relationship in the naming system
indicator_description: Explain the use of kinship terms and their significance in the community.
examplars:
1. Identify and explain the use of various kinship terms in their community. [cite: 52]
2. Discuss the role of kinship terms in establishing social order and respect. [cite: 52]
3. Demonstrate the correct use of kinship terms in various social contexts. [cite: 52]

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B8.1.3.1 Demonstrate an understanding of the relationship between clans and the community
indicator_description: Discuss the relationship between clans and community development.
examplars:
1. Explain how clans contribute to the development of the community. [cite: 52]
2. Identify specific development projects initiated by clans in their community. [cite: 52]
3. Discuss the challenges clans face in contributing to community development. [cite: 52]

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B8.1.4.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the processes and reasons for destoolment/de-skinment of chiefs
indicator_description: Discuss the reasons for destoolment/de-skinment of chiefs in their community.
examplars:
1. Identify and discuss the various reasons why a chief may be destooled/de-skinned. [cite: 52]
2. Discuss the role of the kingmakers and the community in the destoolment process. [cite: 52]
3. Explain the consequences of destoolment on the chief and the community. [cite: 52]

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B8.2.1.1 Demonstrate use of appropriate language in various discourse contexts (Conversation)
indicator_description: Use appropriate language for debating and expressing opinions.
examplars:
1. Identify the language features for debating and expressing opinions in the language. [cite: 52]
2. Plan and participate in a debate on a topical issue. [cite: 52]
3. Use persuasive language and logical reasoning to support an argument. [cite: 52]

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B8.2.2.1 Demonstrate the ability to listen to and evaluate complex oral texts
indicator_description: Analyse and evaluate the speaker's purpose, point of view and use of language in oral texts.
examplars:
1. Listen to a speech or a formal discussion and identify the main argument. [cite: 53]
2. Evaluate the evidence provided by the speaker to support their argument. [cite: 53]
3. Discuss the speaker's use of rhetorical devices and their impact on the audience. [cite: 53]

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B8.2.4.1 Demonstrate knowledge of the function of tone in grammatical structures
indicator_description: Identify and use tone to show grammatical relationships in sentences.
examplars:
1. Identify how tone is used to indicate tense, aspect, or mood in the language. [cite: 53]
2. Produce sentences where tone change indicates a change in grammatical meaning. [cite: 53]
3. Mark tone correctly on words in sentences to reflect intended grammatical meaning. [cite: 53]

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B8.2.6.1 Demonstrate the ability to plan and deliver structured presentations on complex topics
indicator_description: Deliver a structured presentation on a topical issue in the community.
examplars:
1. Research a topical issue (e.g. sanitation, importance of education) and plan a presentation. [cite: 53]
2. Use appropriate visual aids and non-verbal cues to enhance the presentation. [cite: 53]
3. Respond to questions and feedback from the audience effectively. [cite: 53]

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B8.3.1.1 Demonstrate the ability to read, understand and evaluate a variety of complex texts
indicator_description: Analyze and evaluate the content, structure and style of various texts.
examplars:
1. Read a variety of texts (expository, argumentative, creative) and analyze their structure. [cite: 53]
2. Evaluate the author's use of language and stylistic devices. [cite: 53]
3. Discuss the themes and social relevance of the texts. [cite: 53]

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B8.3.2.1 Demonstrate the ability to translate simple texts between English and the Ghanaian Language
indicator_description: Translate simple sentences and short paragraphs accurately, maintaining meaning and tone.
examplars:
1. Identify the key meaning of a simple text in the source language. [cite: 53]
2. Use appropriate vocabulary and grammatical structures in the target language to convey the meaning. [cite: 53]
3. Review the translation to ensure accuracy and flow. [cite: 53]

subject: Ghanaian Language
sub_strand: INTEGRATING GRAMMAR IN WRITTEN LANGUAGE (USE OF NOUNS, PRONOUNS AND ADJECTIVES)
content_standard: B8.4.2.1 Demonstrate advanced use of nouns, pronouns and adjectives in writing
indicator_description: Use various noun and pronoun forms to achieve cohesion and clarity in writing.
examplars:
1. Use reflexive, relative and indefinite pronouns accurately in complex sentences. [cite: 53]
2. Use noun phrases effectively to provide detailed descriptions. [cite: 53]
3. Edit a text to improve clarity and flow through better use of nouns and pronouns. [cite: 53]

subject: Ghanaian Language
sub_strand: INTEGRATING GRAMMAR IN WRITTEN LANGUAGE (VERBS, ADVERBS AND CONJUNCTIONS)
content_standard: B8.4.3.1 Demonstrate advanced use of verbs, adverbs and conjunctions in writing
indicator_description: Use various verb forms, complex adverbs and a variety of conjunctions to create sophisticated sentences.
examplars:
1. Use serial verb constructions accurately where applicable in the language. [cite: 53]
2. Use adverbs and adverbial phrases to provide precise information about time, manner, place, and reason. [cite: 53]
3. Use subordinating conjunctions to create complex sentences showing various relationships (cause/effect, contrast, etc.). [cite: 53]
